items.title: Iran ‘negotiating’ with FIFA over moving World Cup games to Mexico: embassy
items.description: Iran’s participation at this summer’s finals in the United States, Canada and Mexico has been thrown into doubt since the West Asia war began

items.title: Larijani's death would mean 'Iran's leadership very much infiltrated by Israeli intelligence'
items.description: Israel said Tuesday it had killed Iran's powerful national security chief, Ali Larijani, in what would be a huge blow to the Islamic republic more than two weeks into the war engulfing the Middle East. It would also suggest Iran's senior leadership has been infiltrated by Israeli intelligence, says France 24's Angela Diffley, meaning Iran's leaders "can no longer trust anybody".

items.title: Iran says over 50 cultural sites damaged in U.S. and Israeli strikes
items.description: Officials said on Sunday that 56 sites were affected, including museums, historic government buildings, traditional bazaars and mosques across the country. The damage highlights concerns about the impact of military operations on historically significant landmarks.

items.title: Historian shares how Russia benefits from the US being at war with Iran
items.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with historian Sergey Radchenko about how Russia benefits from the U.S. being at war with Iran.

items.title: What does the Iran war mean for the US defense sector?
items.description: US defense companies are being called on to ramp up production to meet fresh demand from the war in Iran. It opens up an existing rift between them and the Trump administration.

items.title: Trump was warned of likely Iranian retaliation on Gulf allies: report
items.description: The U.S. President twice on Monday (March 16, 2026) said that Iran’s retaliatory strikes against Qatar, Saudi Arabia, ‌the United Arab Emirates, Bahrain and Kuwait were a surprise

items.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: U.S. forces ‘no longer need’ military help in the Iran war, says Trump
items.description: Joe Kent, director of the National Counterterrorism Centre, said Iran “posed no imminent threat to our nation, and it is clear that we started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby”; no immediate comment from the White House

items.title: 'Coalition of the unwilling': Allies refuse to clean up Trump's Strait of Hormuz mess
items.description: PRESS REVIEW – Tuesday, March 17: Donald Trump has called on US allies to help the US secure the Strait of Hormuz as the energy crisis intensifies. But after those allies refused to help, Trump promptly threatened NATO. In the midst of the EU's energy crisis, Belgium's prime minister has suggested "normalising" relations with Russia, which sparked outrage in the Belgian press. Plus: Britain scrambles to control a meningitis outbreak and a new letter reveals just how poor Claude Monet was in the early part of his career.

items.title: AFCON 2025 becomes most widely watched edition with 61% global rise
items.description: The 37th edition of the Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON 2025), held in Morocco, has shattered records. Preliminary figures from independent research agencies show a 61% surge in global viewership, confirming the tournament's status as one of the fastest-growing sporting events in the world.

items.title: Migrant workers bear brunt of Iran attacks in Gulf
items.description: Since February 28, Tehran has launched wave after wave of missile and drone attacks against the Gulf states in retaliation for U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran.

items.title: Watch: At UN General Assembly, India slams Pakistan’s ‘air-bombing campaigns’ during Ramadan
Voter turnout down in Congo-Brazzaville as president set to extend rule
items.description: Voters in Congo-Brazzaville went to the polls on Sunday in an election expected to extend the rule of President Denis Sassou Nguesso.

items.title: India news: Second LPG tanker reaches India amid shortage
items.description: LPG-laden vessel Nanda Devi has arrived in western India escorted by the Indian Navy, amid a shortage due to the conflict in Iran. Also, India's UN envoy had sharp words for Pakistan. DW has more.

items.title: Daily Quiz: on lexicons and lives
items.description: Does the joint U.S.-Israeli military operation on Iran constitute a war, conflict, escalation, or campaign change? A quiz on lexicons and lives.

items.title: Quick hits: Sam Kerr provides geometric genius as Katrina Gorry takes a battering
items.description: Sam Kerr gets her angles right, Katrina Gorry stands tall despite a battering and Kaitlyn Torpey has a birthday to remember. Here are the quick hits from the Matildas' exhilirating 2-1 semifinal triumph over China.

items.title: Trump administration cannot implement 'sweeping' funding freeze, U.S. court rules
items.description: The freeze ‌implicated up to $3 trillion in federal funding

items.title: In video message, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu sends Nowruz wishes to Iranians
items.description: "To the brave people of Iran, I wish you, as I do every year a happy holiday season, beginning with the festival of lights," said Benjamin Netanyahu

items.description: Israel claims to have killed Soleimani. Here is what we know about the veteran commander.

items.title: Israel claims killing of top Iranian officials as conflict deepens

items.title: Cristina Fernández de Kirchner goes on attack as she appears in court for ‘Cuadernos’ trial
items.description: Former president summoned for questioning in connection with ‘Cuadernos’ case; She delivers statement attacking President Milei, Judiciary, government, but refuses to answer questions. Leer más

items.title: British photographer Mark Neville on documenting four years of war in Ukraine
items.description: A British photographer who has spent the last four years living under near-daily attacks on Kyiv has told FRANCE 24 that his nervous system has adapted to the war in Ukraine. Mark Neville says he feels he now has an advantage over people living in the rest of Europe, as his body has naturally adapted to the situation. He has set up a charity, Postcode Ukraine, which makes deliveries of humanitarian aid to frontline towns. Neville also takes photos of the people he meets to bring their stories to the wider world as the war drags on, and has had great success with his book "Diary of a Volunteer". He spoke to us in Perspective.

items.title: US can access military data collected by Australian aircraft in Gulf
items.description: The defence minister has confirmed intelligence gathered by an Australian surveillance aircraft deployed to support Gulf states can be accessed by the United States, potentially informing its strikes on Iran.

items.title: Former Belgian diplomat first to stand trial over 1961 murder of Congo’s Lumumba
items.description: A former Belgian diplomat has been ordered to stand trial over the 1961 murder of Patrice Lumumba, who became the first prime minister of Congo after the country’s independence from Belgium. The 93-year-old suspect is the first person to be prosecuted in the case. All other suspects have passed away.

items.title: Watch: US-China summit delayed: Trump cites War, Hormuz tensions
items.description: The move comes as tensions rise over the Strait of Hormuz and global oil supply concerns

items.title: Major banks respond to Reserve Bank cash rate increase
items.description: Australia's four major banks respond to the Reserve Bank of Australia's decision to increase the national cash rate to 4.1 per cent.

items.title: Oil surges 3% as Iranian strikes on UAE renew supply fears
items.description: Oil prices jumped about 3% on Tuesday after renewed Iranian attacks on the United Arab Emirates rekindled supply fears, with the Strait of Hormuz remaining largely shut and several U.S. allies rebuffing President Trump's call to send warships to escort shipping through the vital waterway.

items.title: Appeal trial of 18 Senegalese nationals detained during AFCON final postponed
items.description: The Rabat Court of Appeals has postponed the appeal trial of 18 Senegalese fans conviceted of hooliganism during the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations final. Originally scheduled for Monday, the hearing has been pushed back to 30 March.

items.title: Northern Ireland's Gerry Adams tells court he wasn't in IRA
items.description: The former leader of Sinn Fein is accused in London's High Court of being responsible for Irish Republican Army (IRA) attacks in England. But Gerry Adams said he was never a member, instead calling them his "neighbors."

items.title: Joe Kent, a top counterterrorism official, resigns citing Iran war
items.description: Kent said he "cannot in good conscience" back the Iran war. In his resignation letter, he says Iran "posed no imminent threat to our nation."

items.title: Belgian ex-diplomat to stand trial over Patrice Lumumba death
items.description: A Brussels court has ordered a former Belgian diplomat to stand trial over the 1961 killing of Congolese independence icon Patrice Lumumba. It's a watershed moment for Lumumba's family, who said they are relieved to see the former colonial power "finally confronting its history."

But the true scale of the tragedy remains unclear, as access to information from authorities continues to shrink.

items.title: Trump seeks to delay China visit as Beijing rebuffs Hormuz demands
items.description: US President Donald Trump on Monday said he is seeking to delay a highly anticipated trip to China in early April by about a month because of the Iran war. It comes after Trump called on numerous nations, including China, to help ships safely transit the Strait of Hormuz, a request that so far has largely been rebuffed.

items.description: Carlos Mazon, president of the Valencia regional government, is facing scrutiny over his handling of floods in late October 2024 that killed more than 200 people.

items.description: Is humanity destined for war?

items.title: Could a surprise alliance win Paris mayoral race for Dati?
items.description: The Paris mayoral race is set to be decided in a second round of voting on Sunday. The first round saw the candidate for the right, Rachida Dati, trailing behind left wing candidate Emmanuel Gregoire, but shifting alliances among the parties ahead of the runoff might prove pivotal.

items.title: Belgian court clears way for trial over 1961 killing of Congo PM Lumumba

items.title: Guy Poran urges international community to demand Israeli govt end settler violence in West Bank
items.description: Eve Irvine is pleased to welcome Guy Poran, former Israeli Air Force Pilot, human rights activist and volunteer for Israeli peace activist group Mistaclim. According to Poran, the situation in the West Bank has entered a dangerous phase, following wars in Gaza, Lebanon and Iran. He says the Israeli army is encouraging the escalating attacks by Israeli settlers, both passively and actively. To foster meaningful change, Poran is calling on the international community "to raise hell" and demand the Israeli government end the violence.

items.title: Trump upset as U.S. partners reject call for Hormuz warship escorts
items.description: A number of U.S. partners, including Germany, Spain and Italy, said they had no immediate plans to ‌send ships to help reopen the strategic waterway, which Iran has effectively shut with drones and naval mines

items.title: German Chancellor says Iran war has no military solution
items.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the Iran conflict cannot be resolved militarily and must be resolved politically.

items.title: Argentina finalises withdrawal from World Health Organization after one-year process

items.title: From Haitian roots to viral fame: The rise of performer Ralph Beaubrun
items.description: Dancer, choreographer and singer Ralph Beaubrun has built a global following with his joyful dance classes and infectious energy online. Now the Franco-Haitian artist is stepping further into music with his new EP "ID.2", blending Afro-Caribbean rhythms, shatta and pop while singing in Creole, French and English.

items.title: Trump vows to 'take' Cuba as island hit by nationwide blackout
items.description: - US President Donald Trump vowed Monday to "take" Cuba as the communist island plunged into darkness under a total power blackout linked to a crippling oil embargo imposed by Washington.

items.title: India terms Pakistan’s bombing of drug rehab centre in Kabul a ‘barbaric’ act
items.description: The Omid Drug Addiction Treatment Hospital in Kabul is a former U.S. military base that has been converted into a drug addiction treatment centre for Afghanistan’s drug addicts; the Taliban administration has revealed that the bombing led to the death of 400 people seeking treatment in the facility; MEA spokesperson says the ‘heinous act of aggression by Pakistan is also a blatant assault on Afghanistan’s sovereignty’

items.title: Mexico City breaks Guinness Record with giant football training session

items.title: Previously unseen ancient graffiti reveals scenes of everyday life in Pompeii
items.description: A growing number of researchers are turning to the Pompeii's graffiti to better understand the lives, humor and concerns of ordinary people nearly 2,000 years ago. Spoiler alert: They liked gladiators.

items.title: Top US counterterrorism official resigns over Iran war
items.description: The director of the U.S. National Counterterrorism Center, Joe Kent, has announced his resignation, saying he “cannot in good conscience” back the Trump administration’s war in Iran. Kent said on social media that Iran “posed no imminent threat to our nation, and it is clear that we started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby.”

items.title: Iran's Larijani
items.description: Iran’s security chief Ali Larijani, whom Israel claimed Tuesday to have killed in a strike, was a Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's right hand man and, following the supreme leader’s death early in the war, was believed to be effectively running the country. France 24’s Delano D’Souza takes a look at a man whose death, if confirmed would be a major blow against the Islamic republic.

items.title: Decades of deployment: America's extensive military presence in the Middle East
items.description: As yet another war rages in the Middle East, we take a closer look at the United States' military presence across the region. It's a presence that stretches back decades, but took on heightened visibility during the Iran-Iraq war of the 1980s, when both those nations began targeting oil tankers and merchant shipping vessels in the Arabian Gulf. Some 40,000 to 50,000 US troops are stationed across the region full-time. FRANCE 24's Luke Shrago and David Gilberg explain where they are stationed.

items.title: UAE briefly closes airspace as West Asia conflict escalates

items.title: Iran war: Germany firmly against military role in conflict

items.description: Religious brother Joshy George Pottackal, born in India, is to become the first auxiliary bishop of non-European heritage. The diversity of the global Catholic Church is reaching the German episcopate.

items.title: Iran war: Why gold prices are not soaring
items.description: Gold is typically seen as a safe haven, especially in times of war and other crises. Notwithstanding the ongoing war in Iran, the price is steady. What's driving the stability?

items.title: Kyle Sandilands considering 'many options' amid doubt over radio future

items.title: Afghan govt says 'around 400' killed in Pakistani strike on hospital
items.description: At least 400 people were killed and 250 injured in an air strike by Pakistan on a drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul, a spokesman of the Afghan Taliban government said on Tuesday. Pakistan rejected the claim as false and misleading and said it "precisely targeted military installations and terrorist support infrastructure" on Monday night.

items.title: Insta-savvy churches are propelling Brazil’s Gen Z to the right
items.description: From Instagram to TikTok, young Brazilians are posting Bible verses, attending religious services and proudly identifying as evangelical or Catholic. And with that is emerging a growing alignment with right-wing politics, imbuing a cohort that represents a third of the electorate. Leer más

items.description: As Trump dials up pressure on Europe to guard the Strait of Hormuz, Russia is taking advantage of the rise in energy prices to the detriment of Ukraine and its allies in Europe.

items.title: Remote NT town's only major supermarket set to leave region
items.description: Woolworths has run the main supermarket in the NT town of Nhulunbuy since 1972, but is now planning to pull the pin on its operations.

items.title: Belarus' Kolesnikova receives Charlemagne Prize in Germany
items.description: Belarusian opposition figure Maria Kolesnikova was awarded the Aachen-based prize in 2022 in absentia. She has now collected it after having been released from prison in December.

items.title: Kosovo faces third legislative election in just over a year
items.description: Kosovo’s failure to elect a president has again plunged the nation into political deadlock. Now, it could face its third parliamentary election in just over a year. The country's international reputation is taking a hit.

items.title: No inertia with Persia

items.description: Moldova has imposed a 15-day state of alert in the Dniester river basin as a precaution.

items.title: Somalia warns against Israeli military base plans in Somaliland
items.description: Somalia has warned against alleged Israeli plans to build a military base in the breakaway region of Somaliland, whose independence Somalia does not recognise. The base would be a strategic point for Israel to conduct airstrikes against adversaries in the region as part of its ongoing war.

items.title: Men accused of killing grandmother 'went there for money, not murder'
items.description: A jury sees a covert police recording of Melbourne man Steve Fabriczy admitting to being involved in the burglary where Canberra grandmother Irma Palasics was killed in 1999, as he faces trial for her murder.

items.title: 'Whoever wins the drone war in Ukraine dominates.' Inside the high-tech battle on the Kupiansk front, where every move is watched

items.title: Trump says presidents 'should not have learning disabilities' as he mocks Newsom's dyslexia
items.description: The National Center for Learning Disabilities said it was "disturbed" by the president's remarks.

items.title: France's second homes: The price of a place in the sun
items.description: With the second round of French municipal elections coming up, we take a look at what was a hot-button, campaign issue in many towns and cities: second homes. In some of France’s coastal and Alpine communities, permanent residents are priced out of buying or renting because of vacation homes. We dive into how many second homes France has, who owns them and what mayors are trying to do about some of the issues they can create.
